_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to expand our knowledge of topics, build our understanding of texts, and discuss our ideas with others.

_source.w: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to expand our knowledge of topics, build our understanding of texts, and discuss our ideas with others.
y: Strategic reading helps us learn new information efficiently from any text.
Combining multiple strategies gives us the best possible understanding of complex texts.
Discussing our comprehension with others refines our thinking and reveals new perspectives.
eg: We use visualising to create mental images of complex descriptions or processes.
We make predictions based on text features and prior knowledge, then verify them.
We connect new information in a text to what we already know about the topic.
We summarise key ideas in our own words, distinguishing main ideas from supporting details.
We monitor our understanding and use fix-up strategies when comprehension breaks down.
We discuss our interpretations with others and consider alternative viewpoints.
